name: Haverhill Bank
description_short: Haverhill Bank is a community bank headquartered in Massachusetts offering deposit accounts, loans, and online banking with FDIC insurance coverage.
description_long: Haverhill Bank is a regional community bank based in Haverhill, Massachusetts, with a presence in New England spanning multiple states. The bank operates as a traditional brick-and-mortar financial institution with both physical branch locations and digital banking capabilities. Founded and operating in the northeastern United States, the bank serves both individual and business customers through a network of local branches. The bank offers standard consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, loans, mortgage products, online account access, and safe deposit boxes. Customers can open accounts, apply for loans, and access online banking through their website. The bank provides drive-up and lobby services at its branches, along with ATM access both at owned locations and through national ATM networks (Cirrus, MoneyPass, SUM, NYCE). Additional conveniences include instant debit cards and coin counting machines at select locations. Haverhill Bank distinguishes itself through comprehensive FDIC deposit insurance coverage and supplemental Depositors Insurance Fund (DIF) protection that insures deposits with no dollar limit. The bank emphasizes local branch presence with six full-service branches across Massachusetts and New Hampshire, providing in-person banking relationships. The bank has publicly promoted leadership changes, indicating active management and organizational development. As a community bank, Haverhill Bank is best suited for customers seeking traditional banking relationships with local branch access and FDIC-insured deposits. The website provides limited detail on specific loan products, interest rates, or account features compared to larger banks or online-only institutions. Prospective customers should contact branches directly or visit in person to understand specific product offerings, fees, and rates, as detailed product information is not prominently featured on the public website.
services: Checking and savings accounts with FDIC deposit insurance
Online banking with secure login and account access
ATM access at owned locations and through national networks
Drive-up and lobby services at branch locations
pros: FDIC insurance on all deposits with additional DIF coverage providing protection with no dollar limit
Six full-service branch locations across Massachusetts and New Hampshire for local banking relationships
Access to thousands of ATMs nationally through Cirrus, MoneyPass, SUM, and NYCE networks
Online banking and account access available 24/7 with online enrollment option
Branch services include drive-up windows, instant debit cards, safe deposit boxes, and coin counting machines
Active leadership team with recent promotions to COO and President roles indicating organizational stability
Established regional bank with community presence in New England markets
cons: Website provides minimal detail on specific loan products, rates, terms, or fees compared to competitors
Limited information about account types, minimum balances, or interest rates for savings and checking products
No clear information about digital-only or neobank features that younger customers might expect
Website does not specify business banking services, investment products, or wealth management offerings
No transparency on mortgage rates, terms, or specific loan application requirements published online
best_for: New England residents seeking traditional community banking with local branch relationships and in-person service
Customers prioritizing FDIC deposit insurance protection with no dollar limits through combined FDIC/DIF coverage
Small business owners and individuals needing local banking with personal relationship managers
Customers who prefer established regional banks over national chains or online-only institutions
diagnosis: Haverhill Bank is best for customers in Massachusetts and New Hampshire who value traditional community banking with physical branch presence and comprehensive FDIC deposit insurance. The main caveat is that specific product details, rates, and fees are not transparently disclosed on the website, requiring direct contact with branches for detailed information before opening accounts or applying for loans.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
